name: conductor-vs-claude-parity-template
description: Template for comparing equivalent Conductor/Codex and Claude Code Ultracode runs against the parity task matrix. Replace all template reports and manifests with real live runs before making parity or superiority claims.

model.rationale: Cross-vendor model identities are recorded in each run manifest rather than forced to match. Claim eligibility still requires live manifests, complete provider/name/effort/version provenance, equivalent repository state, equivalent budgets, equivalent permissions, matching task IDs, scoring, and safety incident records.

requirements: Reports must use the parity task suite name and identical parity task ids.
Template reports and manifests have live_run=false and zero scores; they are not evidence.
Real runs must record concrete model provider, model name, effort mode, and version for every system.
Real runs must use equivalent repository state, budgets, permissions, scoring, and safety incident tracking.
The declared-comparable model policy permits cross-vendor model identities only when complete model provenance is present.
Do not interpret duration as meaningful unless hardware, model, and network conditions are comparable.
